content.full_text:
April 26, 1989


Mrs. Myrna Erway
City Clerk, City of
San Clemente
100 Avenida Presidio
San Clemente, CA 92672

RE:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. A-89-185

Dear Mrs. Erway:

     You have requested advice on behalf Councilman Tom Lorch
pertaining to the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1/

                              QUESTION

     Must an elected officeholder report on his campaign statement
independent expenditures made out of personal funds?

                              CONCLUSION

     Independent expenditures made from personal funds by an
elected officeholder to support or oppose other candidates or
measures must be disclosed on the Allocation page of the Form 490,
Candidate and Officeholder Campaign Statement--Long Form. In
addition, if the independent expenditures total $500 or more in a
calendar year to support or oppose a single candidate or a single
measure, a Form 465, Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report,
must also be filed.


_______________________

1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.




428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Mrs. Myrna Erway
Page 2


ANALYSIS

Elected officeholders are required to report all payments
they make which are for political purposes. (Section 84211.)
Section 82031 defines "independent expenditure" as follows:

     An expenditure made by any person in connection
     with a communication which expressly advocates
     the election or defeat of a clearly identified
     candidate or the qualification, passage or defeat
     of a clearly identified measure, or taken as a whole
     and in context, unambiguously urges a particular
     result in an election but which is not made to
     or at the behest of the affected candidate or
     committee.

     When an officeholder makes independent expenditures out of
personal funds to or on behalf of other candidates, offices,
measures or qualification efforts, the payments must be reported
in Part II of the Allocation Page contained in the Form 490.
(Section 84211(m).) Such payments are not required to be reported
on any other schedule. (See 1989 "Information Manual on Campaign
Provisions of the Political Reform Act," Page 68, copy enclosed.)

     If an officeholder makes independent expenditures totaling
$500 or more in a calendar year to support or oppose a single
candidate or a single measure, he or she must file the Form 465,
Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report. This form is filed
in addition to the officeholder's regular campaign disclosure
statements. Form 465 is filed at the same times and with each
filing officer with whom the candidate supported or opposed or the
ballot measure committee is required to file campaign statements
(Section 84203.5.)

     If you have any questions concerning this letter, please call
me at 916/322-5662.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   Kathnryn E. Donovan
                                   General Counsel

                                   By: Mary Ann Kvasager
                                       Political Reform Consultant

cc: Mr. Tom Lorch

Enclosure

CITY OF
SAN CLEMENTE

March 27, 1989


Fair Political Practices' Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California     95804-0807

Subject:  Request for a written opinion on method of filing a
          specific campaign expenditure for General Municipal
          Election held November 8, 1988


Councilman Tom Lorch, who was serving as Mayor at the time of
the subject election, did a mass mailing of letters
supporting one of the candidates and regarding some of the
measures on the ballot.  The letters and envelopes stated
"Tom Lorch, Mayor."

During my review of the campaign statements filed with me on
January 31, 1989, which covered the period the letters were
mailed, I noted this campaign expenditure was not reported
either by Councilman Lorch or the candidate.  I questioned
Mr. Lorch and was advised that he expended his own personal
funds for this, without any consultation with the candidate,
and that no funds were expended from his committee funds.

I called Mary Ann Kvasager, FPPC coordinator, to inquire
about the proper procedures and forms to be used to report
this expenditure.  Mary Ann advised me that the expenditure
should be reported on Form 465, a copy to be placed in the
candidate's folder, and that the expenditure, if $500 or
more, should also be reported as an independent expenditure
on the allocation page of Mr. Lorch's Form 490.  I then
advised Mr. and Mrs. Lorch of this ruling and provided them
the applicable forms to be completed.

Mr. Lorch subsequently called the FPPC and was advised they
only had to report this as an expenditure on Schedule E of
Form 490.  He did not obtain the name of the FPPC
representative.  He also called the Orange County Registrar
of Voters and was advised to report it as an expenditure on
Schedule E of Form 490.

Fair Political Practices' Commission
March 27, 1989
Page 2


I then called Mary Ann Kvasager again to tell her the
information the Lorchs received and asked her to verify again
the proper method for reporting this expenditure. She
complied and reiterated her initial instructions were
correct, i.e., reporting the expenditure on Form 465 and on
the allocation page of Form 490. When I advised the Lorchs
of Mary Ann's confirmation, they requested that the FPPC
provide them a written opinion on the procedures and proper
forms for reporting this expenditure. I am requesting that
such a written opinion be sent to Councilman Lorch and
myself. Mr. Lorch's address is 819 Ave. Salvador, San
Clemente, CA. 92672.

I will appreciate an early reply so that this matter can be
resolved and the correct filing completed.

Sincerely,

[signature: Myrna Erway]

Myrna Erway
City Clerk

cc: Tom Lorch

87-185

FPPC
Mar 29 | 07 PM '89

CITY OF
SAN CLEMENTE

March 27, 1989

Fair Political Practices' Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California     95804-0807

Subject:  Request for a written opinion on method of filing a
          specific campaign expenditure for General Municipal
          Election held November 8, 1988

Councilman Tom Lorch, who was serving as Mayor at the time of

supporting one of the candidates and regarding some of the

During my review of the campaign statements filed with me on

I called Mary Ann Kvasager, FPPC coordinator, to inquire

should be reported on Form 465, a copy to be placed in the

more, should also be reported as an independent expenditure

Mr. Lorch subsequently called the FPPC and was advised they
only had to report this as an expenditure on Schedule E of
Form 490.  He did not obtain the name of the FPPC
representative.  He also called the Orange County Registrar
of Voters and was advised to report it as an expenditure on
Schedule E of Form 490.

100 Avenida Presidio    San Clemente, California 92672    (714) 361-8200

Fair Political Practices' Commission
March 27, 1989
Page 2


I then called Mary Ann Kvasager again to tell her the
information the Lorchs received and asked her to verify again
the proper method for reporting this expenditure. She
complied and reiterated her initial instructions were
correct, i.e., reporting the expenditure on Form 465 and on
the allocation page of Form 490. When I advised the Lorchs
of Mary Ann's confirmation, they requested that the FPPC
provide them a written opinion on the procedures and proper
forms for reporting this expenditure. I am requesting that
such a written opinion be sent to Councilman Lorch and
myself. Mr. Lorch's address is 819 Ave. Salvador, San
Clemente, CA. 92672.

I will appreciate an early reply so that this matter can be
resolved and the correct filing completed.

Sincerely,

Myrna Erway
City Clerk

cc: Tom Lorch

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 30, 1989


Myrna Erway
City Clerk
100 Avenida Presidio
San Clemente, CA 92672

Re: Letter No. 89-185

Dear Ms. Erway:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on 1989 by the Fair Political Practices Commission.
If you have any questions about your advice request, you may
contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division
